mission.title: Install and Configure Openfunction on Kubernetes
mission.description: OpenFunction is a cloud-native Function-as-a-Service platform that allows you to run serverless workloads with ease. Installing OpenFunction enables you to focus on your business logic without the need to manage the underlying infrastructure.

steps.title: Add the OpenFunction Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the OpenFunction Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add openfunction https://openfunction.github.io/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install OpenFunction using Helm
steps.description:
Install OpenFunction in a new namespace called 'openfunction' with the following command:
```bash
helm install openfunction openfunction/openfunction --namespace openfunction --create-namespace --version v1.2.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the OpenFunction pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace openfunction
```

steps.title: Set up monitoring for OpenFunction
steps.description:
Install Prometheus and Grafana for monitoring OpenFunction. You can use the following commands:
```bash
kubectl create namespace monitoring
helm repo add prometheus-community https://prometheus-community.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update
helm install prometheus prometheus-community/prometheus --namespace monitoring
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of OpenFunction will show all pods in the 'openfunction' namespace as running. Additionally, resource limits will be configured, and Prometheus will be set up for monitoring.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add openfunction https://openfunction.github.io/charts
helm repo update
helm install openfunction openfunction/openfunction --namespace openfunction --create-namespace --version v1.2.0

resources:
  limits:
    cpu: "500m"
    memory: "512Mi"
  requests:
    cpu: "250m"
    memory: "256Mi"

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the OpenFunction release from the 'openfunction' namespace using the following command:
```bash
helm uninstall openfunction --namespace openfunction
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volume claims (PVCs) associated with OpenFunction:
```bash
kubectl delete crd $(kubectl get crd | grep openfunction | awk '{print $1}')
kubectl delete pvc --all --namespace openfunction
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'openfunction' namespace and verify its removal:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace openfunction
kubectl get namespaces
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current state of OpenFunction by exporting the configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace openfunction -o yaml > openfunction-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and upgrade OpenFunction to the latest version:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade openfunction openfunction/openfunction --namespace openfunction --version v1.3.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check if the OpenFunction pods are running the new version successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace openfunction
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If the OpenFunction pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for more details:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace openfunction
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If the service is not reachable, verify that the service is running and check the endpoints:
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace openfunction
kubectl get endpoints <service-name> --namespace openfunction
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient resources allocated
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter resource allocation issues, check the resource usage and limits:
```bash
kubectl describe deployment openfunction --namespace openfunction
kubectl top pods --namespace openfunction
```

troubleshooting.title: Helm release not found
troubleshooting.description:
If you receive an error stating that the Helm release is not found, ensure you are using the correct namespace:
```bash
helm list --namespace openfunction
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher, and that Helm and kubectl are installed on your local machine.
